viral_posts.hook: Day 114: How to Not Be a Psychopath
viral_posts.caption:
Day 114: How to Not Be a Psychopath

You don’t trust happiness.

You trust struggle.

A lot of people don’t realize they’ve spent so much time surviving that peace starts to feel suspicious.

The reading says that good feelings can actually make us anxious.

Not because something is wrong.

Because something is right.

And if you’ve spent years carrying stress, chaos, heartbreak, or uncertainty, joy can feel unfamiliar.

So when life finally starts getting better, you start looking for what’s wrong with it.

Maybe happiness isn’t the thing leaving.

Maybe you’re just not letting it stay.

Today’s reading is from The Language of Letting Go by Melody Beattie.

If nobody’s told you this today, I love you. I believe in you. If you want to do hard things, you can do hard things. You got this. And come back tomorrow so we can all be a little less crazy together.

I love y’all.

Peace.

viral_posts.hook: “Gossiping about how I handled the worst year of my life instead of reaching out to see if
viral_posts.caption:
“Gossiping about how I handled the worst year of my life instead of reaching out to see if I’m okay is actually wild.”

I’ve been thinking about that a lot lately.

If you really believed I was struggling… why wasn’t your first instinct to call me?

Why wasn’t it, “Hey man, are you okay? Do you need anything?”

Instead, the people who knew me best just… disappeared.

Some of them talked about me instead of talking to me.

And that’s the part I can’t wrap my head around.

Because if I saw someone I loved going through the hardest season of their life, I wouldn’t make them a conversation.

I’d make sure they weren’t carrying it alone.

That experience taught me something I’ll never forget.

Some people don’t leave because you’re hard to love.

They leave because compassion was never their first instinct.

viral_posts.hook: If she wanted to, she would
viral_posts.caption: If she wanted to, she would

viral_posts.hook: The people who had access to you had no idea how unreachable you are to other people until
viral_posts.caption: The people who had access to you had no idea how unreachable you are to other people until they became other people.

viral_posts.hook: We don’t run to be fit, we run for the fit.
viral_posts.caption: We don’t run to be fit, we run for the fit.

viral_posts.hook: Some people spend so much time asking why their partner isn’t more affectionate, more vuln
viral_posts.caption:
Some people spend so much time asking why their partner isn’t more affectionate, more vulnerable, or more emotionally open.

But very few stop to ask whether they’ve created an environment where those things can actually exist.

Emotional safety isn’t about always saying the right thing. It’s about being the kind of person someone doesn’t have to protect themselves from.

Can they tell you they’re hurting without being judged?

Can they admit they’re scared without being dismissed?

Can they be honest without worrying it’ll be used against them in the next argument?

The softest parts of people don’t come out because they’re demanded. They come out because they’re safe.

Before you ask why someone won’t let their guard down, ask yourself whether you’ve given them a reason to.

If nobody’s told you this today, I love you. I believe in you. If you wanna do hard things, you can do hard things. You got this. And come back tomorrow so we can all be a little less crazy together. I love y’all. Peace.

viral_posts.hook: This is a hard truth I’ve had to admit to myself:
viral_posts.caption:
This is a hard truth I’ve had to admit to myself:

I think every woman I’ve ever loved, I’ve loved more than she loved me.

For years, I thought I just had bad luck.

Now I think I had a pattern.

I was attracted to emotionally unavailable women because the chase felt familiar. Somewhere along the way, I learned that love was something you had to earn.

The crazy part?

When secure, healthy women came into my life, I wasn’t interested. It felt boring. It didn’t create the same spark.

But the spark wasn’t love.

It was anxiety.

I’m finally learning that chemistry and compatibility are not the same thing. And sometimes what feels like love is just an old wound looking for a familiar home.

If nobody’s told you this today, I love you. I believe in you. If you wanna do hard shit, you can do hard shit. You got this. And come back tomorrow so we can all be a little less crazy together. I love y’all. Peace.
